nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to teacher skill and autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI simply as a time saving device

nodes.label: AI as reviewer of teacher-created materials
nodes.grounding_span: I know my students and their needs well enough that I can tell if a suggestion is actually going to be useful

nodes.label: AI suggestions that maintain core learning goals
nodes.label_negative: AI suggestions that reduce challenge or separate students
nodes.grounding_span: without changing the core lesson or the main learning goals

nodes.label: Teacher-created resources as foundation
nodes.grounding_span: all resources are shared between teachers and I think it's really important that these are made, at least initially, by real people

nodes.label: AI use in education
nodes.grounding_span: We have very clear boundaries on how we use AI

nodes.label: AI as threat to student learning assessment
nodes.label_negative: AI as support for student learning
nodes.grounding_span: we as teachers can't properly assess where a student is at education wise because all of their thinking and work is being done by AI

nodes.label: Educational quality and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: we need to know that materials and teaching are sticking to the curriculum

nodes.label: Teacher professional growth
nodes.grounding_span: teachers and support staff know their own material

nodes.label: Child development and inclusive learning
nodes.grounding_span: just because someone has learning difficulties doesn't mean they should be separated out from the rest of the class

nodes.label: Efficiency and reduced workload
nodes.grounding_span: education staff are under a lot of pressure and have a huge workload

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for simple adaptation tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The AI did a really good job of this and saved me a lot of time

nodes.label: Resistance to over-reliance on AI
nodes.grounding_span: I worry that too much reliance on AI will lower the quality of teaching

nodes.label: Preference for AI on simple tasks only
nodes.grounding_span: If I have time, I'll do these tasks myself as I would prefer to not use AI

nodes.label: Resistance to expanding AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I would like to use less of it in the future

nodes.label: Systematic filtering of AI suggestions through contextual factors
nodes.grounding_span: I know my students and their needs well enough that I can tell if a suggestion is actually going to be useful

nodes.label: Systematic task decomposition into specific prompts
nodes.grounding_span: I find its when prompts become more complex that AI struggles
